original text:
Hopp is a rooftop beergarden, located high above the burstling city of Amsterdam.
Not sure if you can hop by? Check our Facebook page for current opening hours.
Enjoy the sunset during sultry summer evenings.
Choose from over 30 different craft beers!
Check out our menu for the most delicious dishes.
Try our own craft beer: Gabbe!
Take the elevator to the 8th floor and enjoy the radiant sun, the beautiful view, the extensive beer menu and the tastiest dishes.
Feel like popping by (alone or with fewer than 10 of your friends)? You're always welcome at Hopp Amsterdam! If you're meeting more than 10 of your besties on Monday - Thursday, let us know in advance. E-mail us at . We do not take reservations for Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays, but everyone is still more than welcome!

We do not take reservations for Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays, but everyone is still more than welcome!

The latest quick edition of the Multi-agent system Self Assessment book in PDF containing 49 requirements to perform a quickscan, get an overview and share with stakeholders.

Who needs to know about Multi-agent system ?
Cloud management for Multi-agent system do we really need one?
Activity Duration Estimates: Explain the four frames of organizations. How can they help Multi-agent system project managers understand the organizational context for their Multi-agent system projects?
Project Schedule: Is there a Schedule Management Plan that establishes the criteria and activities for developing, monitoring and controlling the Multi-agent system project schedule?
Project Portfolio management: If the PMO does not properly balance the portfolio of Multi-agent system projects, who will?
Schedule Management Plan: After initial schedule development, will the schedule be reviewed and validated by the Multi-agent system project team?
Step-by-step and complete Multi-agent system Project Management Forms and Templates including check box criteria and templates.
With this Three Step process you will have all the tools you need for any Multi-agent system project with this in-depth Multi-agent system Toolkit.
This Toolkit empowers people to do just that - whether their title is entrepreneur, manager, consultant, (Vice-)President, CxO etc... - they are the people who rule the future. They are the person who asks the right questions to make Multi-agent system investments work better.

original text:
Does Sunrise Assisted Living Of Westminster offer the following services?
Does Retreat At Church Ranch, The offer the following services?
Does Aspen Place offer the following services?
Does Arbors Of San Marino offer the following services?
Does San Marino Retirement Community offer the following services?
Westminster assisted living communities provide those in the Westminster, CO area a senior housing option that allows for independent living while also providing helpful assistance when needed in a senior community.
Seniors in Westminster can move on from the responsibility of managing and maintaining one's own home and choose to live in one of the 6 Westminster assisted living facilities that offer supervision and assistance with activities of daily living when needed.

Does Aspen Place offer the following services?

original text:
Medical Office Online specializes in Healthcare Information services and has been providing health care providers with unparalleled secure web based access to their medical records for over ten years. We pride ourselves in providing our clients with state-of-the-art medical software while permitting them unlimited access, and top notch data security. The Medical Office Online application is an ongoing development and a product of eight years of design, development and testing, by physicians, computer programmers, security experts, office managers, billers, and medical office personnel. Medical Office Online is a privately held company, headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, USA.
Medical Office Online, Inc. seeks out persons with integrity, who are talented, self motivated, and through continuous self education are willing to cross train in software design, development, marketing, sales, customer training and support.
Your goal in joining Medical Office Online would be to participate in the creation of a new industry by helping Healthcare Providers perform their clinical, financial and regulatory work with state-of-the-art tools.
This position is responsible for providing technical support and remote training.
· Customize training plan for individual practice needs and deliver training to physicians and staff.
· Determine workflow and identify potential problems and involve the correct persons in the solution.
· Provide group and individual training while demonstrating expert knowledge of clinical workflows.
· Work with physicians and staff to understand and research customization requests.
· Provide remote go live support and provide ongoing technical and customer support.

The Medical Office Online application is an ongoing development and a product of eight years of design, development and testing, by physicians, computer programmers, security experts, office managers, billers, and medical office personnel. · Provide remote go live support and provide ongoing technical and customer support.

original text:
I WANT TO SWITCH, BUT HOW DO I DO IT?
1 - Grab your electricity bill. You'll need to have your account number handy.
3 - Fill out some simple information like your name and address.
4 - Power your home on clean, renewable energy. It's that easy!
I WANT TO INSTALL SOLAR, BUT HOW DO I DO IT?

3 - Fill out some simple information like your name, address and current energy bill.
4 - Installation is free! Power your home on clean, renewable energy.
1 - Host a Potluck!
2 - Invite 5 people that didn't see our show.
3 - Ask people what food they will bring.
4 - Follow up with those who are RSVPed with a simple reminder.
5 - Ask people to bring their electricity bill to the potluck.
6 - Discuss your transition out of the fossil fuel cycle and into renewables.
Make the switch to Renewable Energy right now! Even if you live in an apartment building!
HOW TO LET GO OF THE WORLD. Check it out here!
Check out our film GASWORK!

original text:
Narcan is a drug that counteracts an opioid overdose. It is given by nasal spray or injection. For training to receive free Narcan, contact Healthy Streets, 100 Willow St., Lynn @ 339-440-5633 or find them on Facebook. For in-home visits, call Dawn Gaffney @ 781-485-7003. Please pass this life-saving information on to family & friends.
2nd & 4th Wednesdays, 7-10PM in Community Hall.
Sept-June, with additional dates as posted.
Stay tuned for news about singing.
Mondays, 7-8 p.m. in Community Hall.
TBA future dates and times.
This outreach to Saugus school students has been met with great enthusiasm by the town. Bags of donated groceries & supplies are filled on Thursdays by willing churches and given to students on Friday afternoons to take home for the weekend. This free program is offered to students who receive free or reduced-cost meals at school.
 Steve DiChiarra & crew worked hard on rebuilding the damage sustained in the church fire last December. The shed was sided as well as the southern exterior church wall. First & second floor windows were replaced. The storage shed has been cleaned out so that all the Christmas decorations can be put away in there. Some trees in back will be removed and security cameras will be installed. We've been waiting almost a year for this project to be completed & we are grateful that it is almost done.
Open to all Saugus residents every Friday 9:30-11:30AM at Cliftondale Congregational Church, 50 Essex St., Saugus. Our church collects non-perishables for them every first Sunday (Communion Sunday). Please help us fill at least 2 baskets of food every month. Most needed items are: canned soups, canned tuna, canned chicken, spam-type of meats, peanut butter, jelly, American cheese(sliced), 32-oz apple or orange juice, spaghetti sauce, pasta, shelf stable milk(boxed or canned, not refrigerated), mac & cheese, cereal. There is a drop-off location at the office of Century 21, 442 Lincoln Ave, too. If you can make a monetary donation, mail it c/o Cliftondale Congregational. Hungry people are counting on us! If you know anyone in need of food, please let them know about the pantry. If they don't have a way to get there, they can designate a person to pick up for them.
processed_text:
Narcan is a drug that counteracts an opioid overdose. It is given by nasal spray or injection. For training to receive free Narcan, contact Healthy Streets, 100 Willow St., Lynn @ 339-440-5633 or find them on Facebook. For in-home visits, call Dawn Gaffney @ 781-485-7003. Please pass this life-saving information on to family & friends.
2nd & 4th Wednesdays, 7-10PM in Community Hall.
TBA future dates and times.
This outreach to Saugus school students has been met with great enthusiasm by the town. Bags of donated groceries & supplies are filled on Thursdays by willing churches and given to students on Friday afternoons to take home for the weekend. This free program is offered to students who receive free or reduced-cost meals at school.
 Steve DiChiarra & crew worked hard on rebuilding the damage sustained in the church fire last December. The shed was sided as well as the southern exterior church wall. First & second floor windows were replaced. The storage shed has been cleaned out so that all the Christmas decorations can be put away in there. Some trees in back will be removed and security cameras will be installed. We've been waiting almost a year for this project to be completed & we are grateful that it is almost done.
Open to all Saugus residents every Friday 9:30-11:30AM at Cliftondale Congregational Church, 50 Essex St., Saugus. Our church collects non-perishables for them every first Sunday (Communion Sunday). Please help us fill at least 2 baskets of food every month. Most needed items are: canned soups, canned tuna, canned chicken, spam-type of meats, peanut butter, jelly, American cheese(sliced), 32-oz apple or orange juice, spaghetti sauce, pasta, shelf stable milk(boxed or canned, not refrigerated), mac & cheese, cereal. There is a drop-off location at the office of Century 21, 442 Lincoln Ave, too. If you can make a monetary donation, mail it c/o Cliftondale Congregational. Hungry people are counting on us! If you know anyone in need of food, please let them know about the pantry. If they don't have a way to get there, they can designate a person to pick up for them.

original text:
Operating System Windows 8.0 or 8.1 Pro editions in 32-bit or 64-bit, Windows 7 Professional or Ultimate in 32-bit or 64-bit, and Windows Vista Business in 32-bit or 64-bit.
Memory 4 GB minimum for Windows 8, Windows 7, and Windows Vista. The amount of memory should be based on what jobs the PC performs.
Disk Space 100 MB for each local user account on the PC.
Graphics DirectX 9 or later. The newer the graphics card the better.
.Net Framework Full Framework 4.0 or better.
For information on our Best Buy for Business partnership and how you can find STORIS capable hardware at great prices, click here and log into our Client Portal.

original text:
it was the event of the year that i've been anticipating and training hard for. this year, the number of players doubled up, which means even tougher fights for me and everyone else who played last year.
We had to queue to have tea at Hello Kitty Cafe Bangkok. With two cray girls who loves Hello Kitty, the boys had to come along with no rants.
never give up. on your dreams.
it happens as when it happens.
it leaves as when it leaves.
sometimes it happens so fast.
so fast that you're unable to think before reacting.
sometimes it is not how long you've known each other.
because when it is the right time, u just have to hold on.
sometimes it is not how much time you've spent with each other.
because not everyone waits. not every fate waits.
when both held on to each other.
when both appreciates and treasure each other.
when they learn to accept and compromise with no force.
we cross paths on many occasions.
we brushed pass each other at many places.
yet. we did not meet.
it is fate that brings us together.
to have known each other.
may it be a new relationship.
may it be soul mates.
may it be an ending.
it takes us through many new challenges.
some may not pull through it.
others may end up with the ending they always dreamt.
always have faith in it.
because if it's meant to be, it will be.
if it's not meant to be, it will never be even if by force.
two souls walked as one.
venturing on a journey that they own.
two souls loved as one.
with no complaints, with no rage.
appreciating the times that they had.
not letting go when one was falling.
there were many mistakes made.
they did not understand each other's needs and wants.
they expected return. they forgotten on giving and not just receiving.
they forgotten it was a two way communication.
there were barriers. huge barriers that could not be broken.
there were issues. big issues that were never resolved.
they were overlooked as small matters.
unaware that they have both took different paths.
paths that would have led them to a different ending.
it comes to a point where these two souls can no longer hold on together.
with all the mistakes made throughout their journey.
with all the hurt and pain. all the selfishness.
embark on a new journey. when one fate ends, another new journey begins.
always have faith in fate. because fate will bring u to a new adventure.
an adventure that you will look forward to experiencing.
this is the end of a journey of three years.
it has been some time.and not many have heard of it.
memories that will be kept safe.
there will be many WHYs?
heads up. move on. step forward.
there is no point standing at the starting point.
stop dwelling in the past.
time will not turn back.
even if there are or will be regrets.
this is life. but remember. to always believe.
there will be someone out there.
There are times in life when there's something u have to let go although u may not want to. There are times in life when u don't have a choice. There are times in life when u've got to smile and move forward. But the fact that this is life.
Perhaps there are many things in life that you're not willing to let go off. Perhaps you are stuck somewhere in between letting go and holding on. At this point of time, the best thing is to do a little something different from your daily routine. Instead of hot coffee, perhaps a cup of iced tea. Or you could embark on a brand new journey to somewhere new. You might just see things in a whole new different perspective.
Sometimes you have to walk away from what you want to find what you deserve. Sometimes you have to make that decision in life. Even if it is so harsh that you might end up regretting it some day. Even if you have to be the mean and hurtful person. But there are these times when you just have to be that person. Even if it takes to be judged.
There's one thing to always bare in mind, that no matter what you say or do, there will always be someone judging you. Because you can never please everyone in the world.
So stop trying so hard because you don't have to impress the whole world. You just have to be you to take what it takes and do what it makes for your own future. Yes, this may sound a little on the selfish side but basically this is human nature. We tend to be a little selfish especially when it comes to emotional feelings. We put ourselves before anyone else.
Don't let the words, actions and attitude of others affect ur emotion, smile and life. Because you live your life.
And as time goes by.... we travel a little further on a different journey.
We have different mind sets, We took different paths. Without realizing it. At the point of realization, it may be a little too late to turn back time. We've walked our journey differently. We've set our journey differently. Yet we did not realize we were doing it together. Yet there was a huge misunderstanding that it wasn't the way it was expected to turn out.
It is a risk. A risk so high it is worse than forex at any point of time. Yet all of us would take our chances.
The only constant in life is change. Human changes. Their way of living, mindset and feelings change. Some would remain as who they are but most change as they grow older. Some change for the better. Some for the worse. But remember not to change yourself for someone else. Change because you think it is for the best of you. And appreciate every person and moment before anything changes because this is part of life.

original text:
Southampton duo Jan Bednarek and Stuart Armstrong are next up in international action, with both players facing fixtures on Friday night.
For Bednarek, there is a big UEFA Nations League tie, as Poland travel to Italy for their first League A fixture (7.45pm BST).
Poland, who are in action for the first time since the World Cup, also have Portugal in Group 3.
However, Bednarek will have to wait until October for a potential showdown with his Saints teammate, Cédric.
Following tonight's game, Poland will be in friendly action next Tuesday, as they host Republic of Ireland.
For Armstrong, it's a friendly up first, as Scotland host Belgium, at Hampden Park, this evening (7.
The clash with the World Cup's third-placed side comes ahead of Scotland's UEFA Nations League opener, at home to Albania, on Monday.

original text:
After the holiday dinner is served and the gifts have been exchanged, it's time to reach for the remote and hit the recliner to watch a good holiday show with your family and friends. This year, the Hallmark channel is releasing a record 37 new Christmas films and Lifetime has gone all out with a variety of new movies. There's no need to watch repeats in black and white, when you have dozens of new movie options on these channels.
If you already have a Netflix subscription, or were lucky enough to have been given one for the holiday, there are a number of new releases to watch this season.
A Twist of Christmas: December 1 at 8 p.m.
Love for Christmas: December 1 at 10 p.m.
The Christmas Pact December 2 at 8 p.m.
A Star for Christmas: December 2 at 10 p.m.
Christmas Lost and Found: December 7 at 8 p.m.
Santa's Boots: December 8 at 8 p.m.
A Christmas in Tennessee: December 9 at 8 p.m.
Christmas Around the Corner: December 14 at 8 p.m.
Christmas Pen Pals: December 15 at 8 p.m.
Hometown Christmas : December 16 at 8 p.m.
A Christmas Kiss: December 21 at 8 p.m.
A Golden Christmas: December 22 at 8 p.m.
3 Holiday Tails: December 23 at 8 p.m.
So stock up on the eggnog and hot chocolate and sit back and relax!
processed_text:

This year, the Hallmark channel is releasing a record 37 new Christmas films and Lifetime has gone all out with a variety of new movies. There's no need to watch repeats in black and white, when you have dozens of new movie options on these channels.
If you already have a Netflix subscription, or were lucky enough to have been given one for the holiday, there are a number of new releases to watch this season.
Santa's Boots: December 8 at 8 p.m.
So stock up on the eggnog and hot chocolate and sit back and relax!
